source: plan/bearings.md § Article hero art — colorful keyboard SVGs
template: directive: simple line drawing + single splash of color illustrative of subject
subject: Centered vendor hub (a small plum-filled keyboard-shaped node with an inner diamond mark) in the upper-mid canvas, with five arrows fanning out beneath it to a low arc of five product-variant board silhouettes (each a small rounded-rect keyboard outline with faint key-dot rows and tiny config-tag lines beneath); a single plum sparkline rises left-to-right along the bottom of the canvas, peaking at the right edge with the canonical filled-core-plus-outer-ring focal dot — together reading as 'one vendor, many configurator variants, configurator era trending up'.
prompt: Fifth Trends-pillar hero — must read as a sibling of the four existing pieces (ochre 75, steel-blue 215, sage 145, bronze 25) without colliding with any of them. Picked muted plum oklch(0.68 0.105 340): hue 340 sits in the magenta band, a clean 45-degree gap from the nearest existing hue (bronze at 25) and well clear of the green/blue/ochre cluster on the other side of the wheel. Plum also carries a faint 'vendor-brand' / proprietary-product connotation that suits a piece about vendor consolidation, where the chart-y greens and blues of prior pieces would feel too neutral. Composition fuses the brief's two requirements into one frame: hub-and-spoke for the vendor-to-variants relationship up top, sparkline along the bottom for the trends-pillar visual rhythm. The hub is intentionally NOT a generic abstract circle — it's a small keyboard-shaped capsule with a diamond mark inside, which lets the vendor read as 'a vendor that makes keyboards' rather than a generic node graph; the two key-dot hints inside the capsule reinforce the reading at small sizes. Five variants in the arc (not three): five maps to the article's example configurators (case material, mount, weight, color, layout) and gives the fan enough density to read as 'configurator' rather than 'three SKUs'. The variants are deliberately sized in a shallow U — slightly smaller at the edges, slightly larger at center — to imply perspective from the hub above. Each variant has a tiny two-line tag block beneath it (one full-width, one shorter) hinting at config labels without rendering text, so the SVG stays font-free and the metaphor stays language-agnostic. Single-splash convention preserved strictly: the hub is the only filled splash element above the sparkline (matching the 'focal cursor' role in prior pieces), variants and arrows stay warm-grey stroke-only, sparkline carries the splash on stroke + 0.16 area fill + 0.08 band wash. Sparkline parameters mirror the 75-percent-default piece (8 points, lower-left to upper-right, gentle accelerating rise) to anchor the visual family. Sparkline lives in the lower band (y=624→444) rather than crossing the variants — keeping the two readings layered cleanly. Aggregate inked content: hub at y=244-316, variant arc at y=448-568, sparkline at y=444-624 with focal dot at (1110, 444). Width spans x=90-1110 (~85% canvas), height spans y=244-624 (~50% canvas) — slightly more compact vertically than the prior pieces because the hub-and-fan structure benefits from the breathing room above; still well within the brief's ~85%w × ~75%h envelope.
